author: han0110
content: A note on the proof size limitation, currently only ZisK and Risc0 proof fit in 400 KiB (Airbender/SP1 ~1.3 MiB and OpenVM ~800 KiB), will see if there is a quick workaround to config them to produce smaller proofs.

author: taulepton_
content: Ahh good point, I had bumped the CL specs to 400KiB to support zisk - I wasn't mindful of the size of openVM / airbender proofs. Do you think we should take the max of these ~1.3MiB for now and then we can reduce later? cc: <@774033563732541451>

author: han0110
content: I think that’d make the testing easier for sure, but not sure if it makes the network bandwidth situation too far from the actual case

author: jsign
content: Agree with Han. I think we can bump the limit now to not block testing, and as we gain more confidence which are the zkVMs that will be used we can keep adjusting that limit to not get in the way. There is already a target (~300KiB), so in theory things should converge to that value eventually.

author: taulepton_
content: Cool so is 1.3 MiB sufficient for both Airbender and SP1 <@466102668138512394>?

author: han0110
content: Yes as far as I see, both at latest version takes ~1.21 MiB. Can't guarantee they don't change between versions tho, but I think 1.3 MiB is already very generous (if they grow more than that we could probably skip that version).
